page: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 2458 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory operations and database tasks. Large block memory bandwidth tests show read speeds of 207.3 GB/sec and write speeds of 95.23 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. PassMark database operations and extended instructions tests also rank in the top 25%. Most other compute metrics, including stress-ng multi-core performance and Geekbench scores, fall within the average range. However, large-scale LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck, with 70B model inference yielding a poor 2.33 tokens/sec.

The D64ds_v4 offers solid qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads requiring a balance of high core counts, substantial memory, and fast local storage. The inclusion of a 2.4 TB local disk reduces the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. This configuration is well-suited for relational databases, enterprise application servers, data caching layers, and general-purpose web serving at scale.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) running at 2.6 GHz, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong memory performance, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 207.3 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 95.23 GB/sec. Benchmark results place its database operations and extended CPU instructions in the top 25%. While general compute and multi-core performance remain average, the server exhibits poor throughput for large-scale LLM text generation. The D64ds_v4 is highly suitable for database hosting, enterprise application backends, and large-scale general-purpose workloads requiring a balanced allocation of compute, memory, and high-capacity local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v4 is a DDSv4 virtual machine with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, offering strong database and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v4 is a 64-vCPU virtual machine with 256 GB RAM and 2.4 TB local storage, optimized for databases and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 platform combining sixty-four dedicated vCPUs, substantial memory, and high-capacity local storage for demanding enterprise applications.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM
2458 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage support
Strong memory bandwidth performance exceeding 207 GB/sec for large block reads
Top-tier performance in database operations and extended CPU instruction benchmarks
Optimized for relational databases, enterprise application servers, and data caching
